IdTemplate.title: IdTemplate
IdTemplate.description: Represents a template for generating IDs, such as student, employee, or membership cards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ID template.

name.description: Name of the ID template (e.g., 'Student ID', 'Employee Badge').

description.description: A detailed description of the ID template and its purpose.

fields.description: List of required and optional fields for this template (e.g., name, address, date of birth).

layout.description: Defines the layout of the ID template using a data structure like JSON or string.

GeneratedId.title: GeneratedId
GeneratedId.description: Represents a generated ID for a user based on a selected template.

id.description: Unique identifier for the generated ID.

creationDate.description: Date and time when the ID was generated.

fieldValues.description: A JSON string containing the values entered by the user for the template fields.

photoUrl.description: URL of the uploaded photo for the ID.

qrCodeData.description: Data encoded in the QR code associated with the ID.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the IDGenius application.

EventReport.title: EventReport
EventReport.description: Represents a user-submitted traffic event or incident.

location.description: The geographical location of the event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

severity.description: The severity level of the event.

userId.description: The ID of the user who submitted the report.

user.description: The display name of the user who submitted the report.

userAvatar.description: URL to the avatar of the user who submitted the report.

picture.description: URL of an image related to the event, if provided.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the event was created.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made by a user on an event report.

userId.description: The ID of the user who wrote the comment.

author.description: The display name of the user who wrote the comment.

avatar.description: URL to the avatar of the user who wrote the comment.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the comment was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  User documents are accessible only to the user and admins (admin check not modeled here).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores generated ID information for each user. Organized as a subcollection of the user document to ensure that only the user can access these documents. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: generatedId
params.description: The unique identifier for the generated ID.

definition.description: Stores ID templates.  Templates are intended to be created and modified by admins (admin check not modeled here).

params.name: idTemplateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ID template.

definition.description: Stores user-submitted traffic event reports. These are public and can be read by anyone, but can only be created by authenticated users.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the event report.

definition.description: Stores comments for a specific event report. Comments are publicly readable but can only be created by authenticated users.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent event report.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the comment.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support the IDGenius application and the new Kinshasa Flow traffic reporting features. It emphasizes a clear separation between private user data and public-facing community data. User-private data remains locked down, while the new `/events` collection is made public for reading to allow all users to benefit from traffic reports. Write access to `/events` and its `comments` subcollection is restricted to authenticated users (including anonymous ones) to prevent spam and ensure accountability, with rules enforcing that the `userId` field matches the creator's UID.
